summary: Patient Naladala Sreedevi reports a 7/10 headache and episodic vertigo for 7 days with no known triggers.  She has a history of head surgery one year prior due to an accident, with no reported complications.  She takes insulin and metformin (dosage needs clarification). She reports occasional alcohol and tobacco use. No known allergies and no significant family history reported.

patient_data.name: nirvana
patient_data.symptoms: 7/10 headache for 7 days, episodic vertigo (feeling unsteady, approximately 4 times a day), no triggers
patient_data.past_surgeries: Head surgery 1 year prior due to accident. No complications.

followup_notes: Clarified medication dosages and frequency.  Added detail to vertigo symptoms and frequency.
specialist_rationale: The patient presents with a severe, persistent headache (7/10 for 7 days) and episodic vertigo.  Given the history of prior head surgery, these symptoms warrant a thorough neurological evaluation to rule out any complications or underlying neurological conditions.  A neurosurgeon's input is recommended due to the previous head surgery to assess potential links between the surgery and current symptoms.
